description: Refactor agent architecture: Durable Object as source of truth
steps: Move live agent state (balance, openPositions, cooldown, lastDecision) from D1 reads into Durable Object memory
Persist critical state snapshots into DO storage (state.storage)
Reduce D1 reads inside agent-loop to only initialization and history writes
Ensure DO recovers state correctly after restart from storage
Design a DO storage schema versioning strategy (migration helpers for storage shape changes)
Verify agent loop works without direct dependency on D1 for live state
Add tests for DO state recovery and migration edge cases

description: Optimize agent-loop to reduce latency and redundant operations
steps: Cache market data inside Durable Object for short TTL (e.g. 30–60 seconds)
Avoid duplicate OHLCV fetches within same tick window
Parallelize independent async operations (market data, indicators, etc.)
Ensure LLM calls are only triggered when needed (skip if no meaningful change)
Add timing instrumentation to measure before/after latency
Add unit tests for cache hit/miss and skip-decision logic

description: Add WebSocket support for live agent updates
steps: Create WebSocket endpoint in Worker for /api/agents/:id/ws
Use Durable Objects hibernatable WebSocket API (acceptWebSocket) for cost efficiency
Route WebSocket connections to the correct Durable Object instance
Authenticate the WebSocket upgrade request using existing SIWE session (validate session cookie/token before accepting)
Store active connections in Durable Object via getWebSockets()
Broadcast agent state updates (trades, decisions, status) to connected clients
Handle reconnects, cleanup of closed connections, and hibernation wake
Add frontend composable for WS connection with auto-reconnect
Test real-time updates end-to-end

description: Introduce async processing using Cloudflare Queues
steps: Create queue binding and consumer in wrangler.toml
Refactor LLM decision step: DO enqueues job with agentId + prompt context, sets state to 'awaiting_llm'
Implement queue consumer Worker that calls LLM and returns result to DO via stub.fetch()
DO receives result, validates, persists decision, and continues the loop
Handle idempotency: if DO alarm fires again while awaiting_llm, skip re-enqueue
Add retry logic with exponential backoff in queue consumer
Add dead-letter queue handling for permanently failed jobs
Verify agent loop remains stable under load and concurrent alarm/queue-result races

description: Implement global rate limiting using Durable Objects
steps: Create GlobalRateLimiterDO class
Map userId to limiter instance using idFromName
Track LLM usage per user (requests per minute/hour)
Integrate limiter into LLM router
Return proper error (429) when limit exceeded
Test concurrency safety and correctness

description: Improve caching strategy for external data and LLM
steps: Add KV caching layer for market data (short TTL, keyed by pool+timeframe)
Cache LLM responses for identical prompts (optional short TTL)
Add cache invalidation strategy (TTL-based + manual purge endpoint)
Ensure fallback logic works correctly with cached vs stale data
Measure reduction in external API calls

note: AgentManagerDO binding and class export already exist in wrangler.toml and index.ts — this is a refactor of existing scaffolding, not a greenfield build
steps: Audit existing AgentManagerDO class and bindings to understand current state
Refactor cron handler to delegate scheduling to AgentManagerDO instead of querying D1 directly
Move agent scheduling logic (interval matching, active agent tracking) into Manager DO
Maintain a cached list of running agents in DO storage to avoid full DB scans each cron tick
Add/remove agents from the Manager's tracking list on start/stop/delete API calls
Verify scheduling accuracy and performance vs current cron-based approach

description: Improve error handling and retry logic in agent loop
steps: Wrap LLM calls with retry + exponential backoff
Handle external API failures gracefully (Gecko, DexScreener) with structured error types
Persist pending actions to DO storage for retry across alarm cycles
Add structured error logging with consistent format (error code, context, recoverable flag)
Ensure agent loop never crashes silently — always record failure reason in D1 decisions log
Add tests for each failure mode (LLM timeout, API 429, malformed response, network error)

description: Add logging, metrics, and debugging tools
steps: Standardize structured logging format across all services
Log key events: decisions, trades, errors, retries, self-modifications
Add timing metrics for LLM calls, market data fetch, indicator computation
Expose debug endpoint for agent DO state (GET /api/agents/:id/debug, gated to admin/tester role)
Verify logs are readable and useful in wrangler tail and production
Add per-IP fallback rate limiting (Workers-level, before auth)
Validate all API inputs with shared schemas (audit for any unvalidated endpoints)
Harden internal endpoints — verify service binding enforcement, reject direct Worker URL access
Audit auth flow and session validation (SIWE nonce reuse, session expiry, cookie flags)
Test for common abuse scenarios (replay attacks, session fixation, malformed payloads)

description: Expand test coverage for critical paths
steps: Add unit tests for agent-loop decision flow (mock LLM, mock market data, verify trade execution logic)
Add unit tests for self-modification validation and application logic
Add unit tests for persona resolution fallback chain
Add integration tests for prompt-preview parity (API output matches agent-loop prompt construction)
Expand Playwright e2e: agent lifecycle (create → configure → start → verify status → stop → delete)
Add vitest tests for shared package (schema validation edge cases, prompt builder output)
Set up CI coverage reporting

description: Structured schema versioning for DO storage and D1
steps: Define a version field in DO storage root for storage schema versioning
Create migration runner that runs on DO initialization (similar to D1 Drizzle migrations)
Extract inline config migration/clamping logic from agent-loop into dedicated migration module
Document migration strategy for both D1 (Drizzle) and DO storage in a single place
Test forward migration and verify backward-incompatible changes are caught

description: Refactor Ralph loop into structured orchestrator (Node.js)
note: Meta-task: this modifies the orchestrator that runs other tasks, not Heppy Market itself
steps: Replace bash loop with Node.js orchestrator script
Split logic into planner, executor, verifier modules
Implement task selection logic separately from execution
Persist agent progress in structured JSON instead of plain text
Verify loop stability across multiple iterations

description: Implement structured AI coding agent workflow
note: Meta-task: improvements to the Claude Code workflow, not Heppy Market application code
steps: Separate prompts for planning and implementation
Add explicit state machine (planning, implementing, verifying, fixing)
Implement diff-based code generation instead of full file rewrites
Add automatic error feedback loop (retry on failure)
Limit context to only relevant files per task
Verify improved consistency of generated code

description: Prototype Coding Agent using Durable Objects
note: Meta-task / experimental: exploring DO-based autonomous coding agents on edge
steps: Create CodingAgentDO class
Store task progress and state inside DO
Trigger iterations using alarms
Integrate LLM calls into DO workflow
Persist results and logs
Evaluate feasibility of running autonomous coding agents on edge
